what_it_does: Defines an HTML render proof page that loads surface runtime scripts, builds stub channel data, converts it through DNA.faceRecord.channelGraph, renders the channel-view archetype, and mounts the resulting card into the page.
observations: Loads Plus Jakarta Sans and IBM Plex Mono from Google Fonts.
Uses local surface CSS for piece and phone styling plus a small inline body and mount style.
Contains a raw six-channel stub shaped like an /api/channels-shape response.
Creates a channel graph via DNA.faceRecord.channelGraph(raw).
Passes a DNA.org.hubNetwork visualDevice configured with cg.nodes, octagon hub, fabric hub label, weighted organic layout, and 560 by 400 dimensions.
standouts: The page is an executable fixture for proving adapter-to-archetype rendering.
The raw channel data models a lead lane with member lanes and activity counts.
The visual layer is explicitly configured as a hub network rather than left implicit.
conventions: Uses a minimal proof-page structure with #mount as the only body content.
Keeps render setup in an inline async IIFE.
Names the fixture with a leading underscore under pieces/.
Relies on globally available DNA runtime objects from script tags.
Uses fixed visual dimensions inside the render options.
concerns: No fetch or render error handling is present.
The raw channel fixture is hard-coded in the page script.
Runtime dependencies are loaded as globals, so missing scripts would fail at execution time.
The hub network dimensions are fixed, which may constrain responsive behavior from this file alone.
notes: This file is a standalone channel-view render proof using stubbed channel-shape data and the DNA rendering runtime.
questions: What exact schema does /api/channels-shape provide beyond the fields represented in the stub?
Is the lead channel's members/coordinator shape required by channelGraph or only illustrative?
Should the 560 by 400 visualDevice size adapt to viewport or archetype layout?
What should happen if /dna/layouts.json fails to load?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated render-proof purpose matches the file: it proves bind and render of channel-shaped data into a channel-view archetype using a hub-network visual.
intention_signals: It seems intended to demonstrate that raw channel-shape data can be adapted into a graph face record and rendered through the channel-view archetype with an explicit network visual device.
novelty: The file combines a channel-shape data stub, a face adapter, an archetype renderer, and an organic weighted hub network configuration in one small proof page.
contribution: This file contributes a compact executable fixture for channel-view rendering, including representative channel data, the adapter invocation, the archetype render call, and a concrete hub-network visual configuration.
summary_for_embedding: HTML proof page that loads DNA surface runtimes, adapts six stub channels into a channel graph, renders the channel-view archetype, and mounts a hub-network card.
intention_for_embedding: Created to verify the binding and rendering path from channel-shaped data through DNA.faceRecord.channelGraph into a channel-view archetype visualization.
